title: Dense Dutch Navy Bean Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Dutch dish featuring dried white navy beans, perfect for a crowd, this stew is cooked in individual mini cocottes and served in stone bowls, making it a unique and satisfying meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot, saute the chopped onion, carrot, and celery in a little bit of oil until the vegetables are tender.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the soaked and drained navy beans, vegetable broth, tomato paste, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Divide the stew among individual mini cocottes, leaving about 1 inch of space at the top.
Cover the cocottes with lids and transfer them to the preheated oven.
Bake the stew for 45 minutes, or until the beans are tender and the liquid has thickened.
Remove the cocottes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
